pattern: Two-pointer swap: maintain one pointer for even indices needing an odd value and one for odd indices needing an even value, swapping them pairwise until both advance past each other.
hints: Because exactly half the values are even and half are odd, every misplaced even and every misplaced odd have a counterpart to swap with.
Walk an even-index pointer and an odd-index pointer independently, pausing each when it holds a wrong-parity value, then swap and advance both.
Start with i=0 (even index) and j=1 (odd index); advance i by 2 until nums[i] is odd, advance j by 2 until nums[j] is even, then swap and repeat.

edge_inputs.description: already valid interleaving, no swaps needed
fully reversed parity, every element needs to move
minimum length — two elements, one even one odd
minimum length — odd comes first but must move to index 1
repeated values — permutation check must use counts not sets
all even and odd values are the same number

stress.description: large even-length array with equal even and odd counts
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    half = size // 2
    evens = [rng.randrange(0, 501) * 2 for _ in range(half)]
    odds = [rng.randrange(0, 500) * 2 + 1 for _ in range(half)]
    arr = evens + odds
    rng.shuffle(arr)
    return [arr]
